themes.topic: Memory Price Surge Forecast Amid AI Data Center Boom
themes.summary: Jefferies forecasts a significant surge in memory prices, with a 50% increase in Q3 2026 and another 40% in Q4, expecting no relief until 2028, as AI sales are seen justifying the data center spending boom.

mentions.text:
Memory investors receiving a dopamine bomb this weekend. Cheeky DMs from Kawz and BullofBritain today:

> @BULLOFBRITAIN: BLOOMBERG: AI SALES START TO JUSTIFY DATA CENTER SPENDING BOOM

> @KawzInvests: Jefferies Warns Memory Prices Will Surge 50% in Q3 2026 and Another 40% in Q4,

themes.topic: HBM/DRAM as Primary Bottleneck for LLM Inference
themes.summary: The adoption of open-source LLMs by enterprises is accelerating, with quantization reducing TCO for self-hosted inference; however, HBM capacity and DRAM remain the primary constraints and durable demand drivers for the GAI hardware stack.

mentions.text: $NVDA $MU $SNDK $LITE If you are investing in the GAI infrastructure trade, the write-ups in this thread are worth reading closely. No other investor on FinX is standing up their own LLM inference stacks, benchmarking them directly, and sharing the operational learnings in real t

themes.summary: TSMC is partnering with Winbond Electronics for DRAM memory wafers and Wafer-on-Wafer (WoW) advanced 3D stacking technology, diversifying its memory sourcing beyond SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron for AI chips.

mentions.text:
TSMC has turned to Winbond Electronics for DRAM memory wafers as shortage continues, media report:

-Winbond to provide DRAM wafers to be stacked on TSMC logic wafers

-Work together on Wafer-on-Wafer (WoW) advanced 3D wafer stacking

-Currently, TSMC WoW uses memory from SK Hyni

>>TSMC Teams Up with Winbond to Build a Homegrown DRAM Supply Chain for AI

themes.summary: SK Hynix and Samsung are reportedly planning $1.3 trillion in spending over the next decade, doubling previous estimates, indicating significant investment in their semiconductor operations.

themes.topic: DRAM as the Critical Bottleneck for AI, Elon's Focus
themes.summary: Industry experts, including Elon Musk, emphasize DRAM as the most critical bottleneck for AI, with Musk reportedly prioritizing memory for Terafab development, validating its strategic importance.

mentions.text:
Listen to what @GavinSBaker said on All-In at 1:04:06:

“DRAM is the most important bottleneck, and Elon is focusing the Terafab on memory because he believes memory is the most important bottleneck. Not lasers, not capacitors, not power semis, not NAND, not HDDs. DRAM.”

themes.topic: SGLang vs. vLLM Performance for GLM-5.2 NVFP4 Inference
themes.summary: A benchmark of GLM-5.2 NVFP4 inference engines shows SGLang outperforming vLLM for 32K-256K context workloads in speed and latency, while vLLM demonstrates superior stability for 512K/768K long-context tasks due to its CPU KV-offload capability.

mentions.text:

themes.topic: China's AI Efficiency & Open-Source Model Threat
themes.summary: China is seen potentially winning the AI race by generating more intelligence per dollar of capex, with competitive open-source models like GLM-5.2 matching US counterparts at a fraction of the cost, leading to US hyperscalers cutting AI spending and concerns about IP theft.

mentions.text:
China might very well be winning the AI race considering intelligence created per dollar of capex spend.

themes.topic: China's Zhipu AI Matches Anthropic in Cybersecurity
themes.summary: China's Zhipu AI has reportedly matched Anthropic's Mythos in cybersecurity benchmarks, raising concerns about IP theft and the need for stronger guardrails to prevent model distillation, effectively resetting aspects of the AI race.

themes.topic: Rapid Growth & Investment in Robotics Sector
themes.summary: The robotics sector is experiencing rapid growth, with deal values expected to increase tenfold, attracting significant private capital into companies like Saronic, Skild AI, and Apptronik, and creating public investment opportunities in upstream suppliers and pure-play robotics firms.

themes.topic: South Korea's Crucial Role in Humanoid Robotics Supply Chain
themes.summary: South Korea is projected to supply 30% of humanoid components by 2035, leveraging its automotive industry expertise to become a leading non-Chinese supplier of critical actuators, which constitute about 50% of a humanoid's BOM.

mentions.text:

themes.topic: Automotive Suppliers' Role in Humanoid Robotics BOM
themes.summary: Automotive suppliers like Schaeffler are becoming crucial for humanoid robotics, covering up to 50% of a humanoid's Bill of Materials with components like actuators and sensors, with humanoids and AI auto expected to be a significant catalyst post-2027.

themes.summary: Lumentum's EML roadmap may transition from 200G to 300G before 400G, potentially altering demand timing, technology transition pace, and Lumentum's competitive standing in the optical market.

mentions.text:
This article is a deep dive on $LITE, but in many ways, it can also serve as a useful lens for understanding the broader optical market.

Recent industry discussions suggest that Lumentum’s EML roadmap may not move directly from 200G to 400G, but could go through 300G in between.

themes.topic: TSMC-ShunSin Collaboration on COUPE Optical Engine
themes.summary: TSMC is partnering with ShunSin Technology for Fiber Array Unit (FAU) and Grating Coupling work within its COUPE optical engine platform, with ShunSin reporting strong growth, capex plans, and advancements in CPO production.

mentions.text:
TSMC has tapped ShunSin Technology, a Foxconn chip packaging & testing subsidiary, for Fiber Array Unit (FAU) and Grating Coupling work as part of its COUPE optical engine platform, media report, citing ShunSin Chairman Shang-Yi Chiang, former head of R&D at TSMC.
